content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun`
Document lines: 501-539

### 3.9 `MemoryRun`

Audit trail и debug слой.

Нужен, чтобы видеть:
- какой prompt сработал
- на какой модели
- что именно было retrieved
- какой budget был выделен
- какие ops были предложены
- какие ops реально применились
- где pipeline сломался

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `run_id`
- `namespace_id`
- `run_type: route | retrieve_update | canonicalize | construct_notes | reconcile_notes | extract_claims | reconcile_claims | update_profile | update_tasks | consolidate_notes | repair_memory | apply_retention | read_plan | compose_answer | compact`
- `trigger_mode: hot_path | background | manual | slow_path`
- `source_ids_json`
- `retrieved_item_ids_json`
- `retrieval_budget_json`
- `prompt_name`
- `prompt_version`
- `model_name`
- `input_hash`
- `output_json`
- `applied_ops_json`
- `repair_actions_json`
- `latency_ms`
- `token_input`
- `token_output`
- `status: success | failed | partial`
- `error_text`
- `created_at`

Инварианты:
- run log не должен исчезать silently
- `input_hash` полезен для идемпотентности и retry safety

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=`MemoryRun` role as audit/debug trail for pipeline observability summary=In section 3.9, `MemoryRun` is defined as the audit and debugging layer used to inspect pipeline execution details, including prompt/model usage, retrieved context, allocated budget, proposed vs applied operations, and failure points. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<0> keywords=MemoryRun,audit trail,debug layer,pipeline observability tags=data_model,memoryrun,doc_digest evidence=### 3.9 `MemoryRun` Audit trail и debug слой. Нужен, чтобы видеть: - какой prompt сработал - на какой модели - что именно было retrieved - какой budget был выделен - какие ops были предложены - какие ops реально применил...[truncated 27 chars]
[1] type=DIRECTION title=`MemoryRun` schema guidance and invariants (`run log` retention, `input_hash` idempotency) summary=Section 3.9 provides recommended `MemoryRun` fields (identity, run metadata, retrieval inputs, prompt/model versions, outputs, applied/repair ops, latency/tokens, status/errors, timestamp) and defines invariants: run logs must not disappear silently, and `input_hash` supports idempotency and retry safety. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<0> keywords=MemoryRun fields,run_type,trigger_mode,input_hash,idempotency tags=schema_guidance,invariants,memoryrun evidence=Рекомендуемые поля: - `run_id` - `namespace_id` - `run_type: route | retrieve_update | canonicalize | construct_notes | reconcile_notes | extract_claims | reconcile_claims | update_profile | update_tasks | consolidate_no...[truncated 563 chars]

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Source` component role and invariants in v3 data model summary=In section 3.1, `Source` is defined as the raw knowledge/evidence layer that stores chat/tool/imported/external records and supports re-check/explainability; it should be immutable in practice, keep `observed_at` distinct from ingestion time, use `content_hash` for dedupe, and enforce explicit deletion policy. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.1 `Source` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Source,evidence layer,observed_at,content_hash,delete policy tags=data_model,memory_v3,source_component useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 memory model layers and design goals summary=The v3 data model is designed to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability by splitting memory into layers: Source (evidence), Entity/Claim (semantic graph), Note (intermediate), Profile/Task/Episode (operational projections), and MemoryRun (audit). scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=v3 data model,memory layers,typed memory,lineage,temporal truth tags=data_model,architecture,memory_v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Cross-cutting policy: scope, retrieval lifecycle, and usage telemetry summary=In v3, scope and retrieval lifecycle are explicit cross-cutting concerns: use dual scope fields (`scope_text` for explainability and `scope_json` for retrieval/reconciliation), keep `truth status` separate from `retrieval_state`, apply retrieval states active/warm/cold/archived/deleted, and track usage fields (`use_count`, `last_used_at`, `last_validated_at`) to support maturity, retention, and re...[truncated 5 chars] scope=Applies to cross-cutting fields defined in '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля' keywords=scope_text,scope_json,retrieval_state,truth status,usage fields tags=cross_cutting,retrieval_lifecycle,schema_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Note` entity role, boundaries, and invariants in v3 summary=`Note` is an intermediate semantic memory fragment used for important retrieval-useful context that should not yet be hardened into final claims; it excludes source records and chain-of-thought, and follows invariants like one semantic fragment per note and self-contained meaning. scope=Applies to section 3.4 `Note` in 02. Data Model (agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md) keywords=Note,intermediate memory,retrieval,invariants,candidate_claims_json tags=data_model,note_semantics,v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Entity` as canonical memory node with alias/merge semantics summary=In section 3.2, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies mentions of the same object and anchors claims/tasks/notes/episodes; it uses aliases and merge status while preserving history, keeping aliases pointing to the current canonical entity, and allowing erroneous merges to be reversed via audit trail. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Entity,canonical node,entity_aliases,merge,audit trail tags=data_model,memory_v3,entity_component useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`PredicateCatalog` is a reconciliation config, not a memory item summary=In the v3 data model, `PredicateCatalog` is defined as configuration for reconciliation logic rather than a stored memory item; it encodes per-predicate rules such as cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict handling, and projection sync flags. scope=Applies to section 3.3 `PredicateCatalog` in 02. Data Model (agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md) keywords=PredicateCatalog,reconciliation,cardinality,temporal_policy,conflict_policy tags=data_model,reconciliation_config,v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<6> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Episode` is a post-MVP optional memory for reusable lessons, not reasoning traces summary=Section 3.8 positions `Episode` as an optional (non-MVP) memory type for transferable experience—successful tactics, failure patterns, and reusable lessons—with an explicit boundary to avoid storing raw chain-of-thought and full reasoning traces. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.8 `Episode` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Episode,post-MVP,reusable lessons,chain-of-thought boundary tags=data_model,episode,lessons_learned,memory_boundary useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<7> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Profile` is a compact, rebuildable projection layer summary=In section 3.6, `Profile` is defined as an always-in-context projection, not a source of truth, built from explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context; it should stay short, prioritize explicit facts, keep disputable facts in claim/note, and remain rebuildable. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.6 `Profile` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Profile,projection layer,always-in-context,rebuildable tags=data_model,profile,doc_digest,v3 useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
